steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that the house is 'remote and solitary.' The answer must supply textual evidence of isolation — a description that visually shows nothing else around it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — in Oz, "[Dorothy and her companions] passed through the rest of the forest in safety, and when they came out from its gloom saw before them a steep hill, covered from top to bottom with great pieces of rock.": describes Oz, not Kansas — irrelevant to the setting of the house. ✗
  • Choice B — in Oz, "Dorothy fell asleep only once, and then she dreamed she was in Kansas, where Aunt Em was telling her how glad she was to have her little girl at home again.": also describes Oz, not the Kansas landscape. ✗
  • Choice C — in Kansas, "Dorothy lived in the midst of the great Kansas prairies, with Uncle Henry, who was a farmer, and Aunt Em, who was the farmer's wife.": names residents of the house but shows no isolation from surroundings. ✗
  • Choice D — in Kansas, "When Dorothy stood in the doorway and looked around, she could see nothing but the great gray prairie on every side. Not a tree nor a house broke the broad sweep of flat country.": depicts 'nothing but the great gray prairie on every side' and 'not a tree nor a house' — directly evidencing barrenness and isolation. ✓
D is correct.
